items.prompt: overnight-queue-drain runs hourly 00:00-07:00 but multiple instances are landing within the same window and writing .work-queue.json concurrently. The 2026-06-14 ~08:30 run read the file mid-write from a sibling and hit json.JSONDecodeError (Extra data, char 94267); it re-read clean a moment later (41 items). Same-day drain-log shows runs at 05:30, 06:30, 07:06, 08:34 plus this one, several past the 07:00 window bound. Risk: two instances doing read-modify-write on the same JSON with no lock will eventually drop an item or ship a truncated queue (the _note history already records repeated FUSE-truncation repairs). Fix options: (a) single-flight guard - a lockfile/PID check at drain start so a second instance no-ops; (b) tighten the schedule so only one instance can be in-flight; (c) make every queue write go through a read-verify-CAS (compare last-known bytes, retry on mismatch). Decide and wire one.

items.prompt: Recurring FUSE null-padding of status/data/*.json. items.body: MED observability + LOW reliability (bundled). scripts/start-all.ps1 runs node + cloudflared with -WindowStyle Hidden, no logging, and health-checks only `Get-NetTCPConnection -State Listen -LocalPort 4517` (start-all.ps1:13-17). So a silent crashloop is invisible and a wedged-but-listening process never relaunches. Add: (1) a timestamped line appended to a rotating log on each relaunch; (2) a cheap GET / liveness probe with short timeout that relaunches on non-200 as well as no-listener; (3) a tunnel last-seen/up field in status/data/Agents.json via write-dashboard-status.ps1 so a silent outage shows on yesandeverything.com/status.
